classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXXVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psalm to David himself, on the remembrance of the Sabbath.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth this recollection of the Sabbath mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this Sabbath?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is with groaning that he “calls it to recollection.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You have both heard already when the Psalm was read, and you will now hear it when we shall go over it, how great is his groaning, his mourning, his tears, his misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But happy he who is wretched after this manner!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the Lord also in the Gospel called some who mourn blessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How should he be blessed if he is a mourner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How blessed, if he is miserable?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay rather, he would be miserable, if he were not a mourner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such an one then let us understand here too, calling the Sabbath to remembrance (viz.), some mourner or other: and would that we were ourselves that “some one or other”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is here some person sorrowing, groaning, mourning, calling the Sabbath to remembrance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Sabbath is rest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doubtless he was in some disquietude, who with groaning was calling the Sabbath to remembrance.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, rebuke me not in Thine indignation; neither chasten me in Thy hot displeasure” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it will be that some shall be chastened in God’s “hot displeasure,” and rebuked in His “indignation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And haply not all who are “rebuked” will be “chastened;” yet are there some that are to be saved in the chastening.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So it is to be indeed, because it is called “chastening,” but yet it shall be “so as by fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are to be some who will be “rebuked,” and will not be “corrected.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he will at all events “rebuke” those to whom He will say, “I was an hungred, and ye gave me no meat.”… “Neither chasten me in Thy hot displeasure;” so that Thou mayest cleanse me in this life, and make me such, that I may after that stand in no need of the cleansing fire, for those “who are to be saved, yet so as by fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, but because they “build upon the foundation, wood, stubble, and hay.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they should build on it, “gold, silver, and precious stones;” and should have nothing to fear from either fire: not only that which is to consume the ungodly for ever, but also that which is to purge those who are to escape through the fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said, “he himself shall be saved, yet so as by fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because it is said, “he shall be saved,” that fire is thought lightly of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all that, though we should be “saved by fire,” yet will that fire be more grievous than anything that man can suffer in this life whatsoever.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now on what ground does this person pray that he may not be “rebuked in indignation, nor chastened in hot displeasure”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: (He speaks) as if he would say unto God, “Since the things which I already suffer are many in number, I pray Thee let them suffice;” and he begins to enumerate them, by way of satisfying God; offering what he suffers now, that he may not have to suffer worse evils hereafter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thine arrows stick fast in me, and Thy hand presseth me sore” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is no soundness in my flesh, from the face of Thine anger” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has now begun telling these evils, which he is suffering here: and yet even this already was from the wrath of the Lord, because it was of the vengeance of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Of what vengeance?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which He took upon Adam.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For think not that punishment was not inflicted upon him, or that God had said to no purpose, “Thou shalt surely die;” or that we suffer anything in this life, except from that death which we earned by the original sin.…Whence then do His “arrows stick fast in” him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The very punishment, the very vengeance, and haply the pains both of mind and of body, which it is necessary for us to suffer here, these he describes by these self-same “arrows.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of these arrows holy Job also made mention, and said that the arrows of the Lord stuck fast in him, whilst he was labouring under those pains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are used, however, to call God’s words also arrows; but could he grieve that he should be struck by these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words of God are arrows, as it were, that inflame love, not pain.…We may then understand the “arrows sticking fast,” thus: Thy words are fixed fast in my heart; and by those words themselves is it come to pass, that I “called the Sabbath to remembrance:” and that very remembrance of the Sabbath, and the non-possession of it at present, prevents me from rejoicing at present; and causes me to acknowledge that there “is neither health in my very flesh,” neither ought it to be so called when I compare this sort of soundness to that soundness which I am to possess in the everlasting rest; where “this corruptible shall put on incorruption, and this mortal shall put on immortality,” and see that in comparison with that soundness this present kind is but sickness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither is there any rest in my bones, from the face of my sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is commonly enquired, of what person this is the speech; and some understand it to be Christ’s, on account of some things which are here said of the Passion of Christ; to which we shall shortly come; and which we ourselves shall acknowledge to be spoken of His Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how could He who had no sin, say, “There is no rest in my bones, from the face of my sin.”…For if we were to say that they are not the words of Christ, those words, “My God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me?” will also not be the words of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there too you have, “My God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The words of mine offences are far from my health.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as here you have, “from the face of my sins,” so there also you have, “the words of my offences.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if Christ is, for all that, without “sin,” and without “offences,” we begin to think those words in the Psalm also not to be His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is exceedingly harsh and inconsistent that that Psalm should not relate to Christ, where we have His Passion as clearly laid open as if it were being read to us out of the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there we have, “They parted My garments among them, and cast lots upon My vesture.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why should I mention that the first verse of that Psalm was pronounced by the Lord Himself while hanging on the Cross, with His own mouth, saying, “My God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did He mean to be inferred from it, but that the whole of that Psalm relates to Him, seeing He Himself, the Head of His Body, pronounced it in His own Person?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now when it goes on to say, “the words of mine offences,” it is beyond a doubt that they are the words of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then come “the sins,” but from the Body, which is the Church?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because both the Head and the Body of Christ are speaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do they speak as if one person only?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “they twain,” as He hath said, “shall be one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This” (says the Apostle) “is a great mystery; but I speak concerning Christ and the Church.”…For why should He not say, “my sins,” who said, “I was an hungred, and ye gave Me no meat; I was thirsty, and ye gave Me no drink; I was a stranger, and ye took Me not in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I was sick and in prison, and ye visited Me not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Assuredly the Lord was not in prison.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why should He not say this, to whom when it was said, “When saw we Thee a hungred, and athirst, or in prison; and did not minister unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He replied, that He spake thus in the person of His Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Inasmuch as ye did it not unto one of the least of Mine, ye did it not unto Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why should He not say, “from the face of my sins,” who said to Saul, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me,” who, however, being in Heaven, now suffered from no persecutors?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But just as, in that passage, the Head spake for the Body, so here too the Head speaks the words of the Body; whilst you hear at the same time the accents of the Head Itself also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet do not either, when you hear the voice of the Body, separate the Head from it; nor the Body, when you hear the voice of the Head: because “they are no more twain, but one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is no soundness in my flesh from the face of thine anger.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps God is unjustly angry with thee, O Adam; unjustly angry with thee, O son of man; because now brought to acknowledge that thy punishment, now that thou art a man that hath been placed in Christ’s Body, thou hast said, “There is no soundness in my flesh from the face of Thine anger.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Declare the justice of God’s anger: lest thou shouldest seem to be excusing thyself, and accusing Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Go on to tell whence the “anger” of the Lord proceeds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is no soundness in my flesh from the face of Thine anger; neither is there any rest in my bones.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He repeats what he said before, “There is no soundness in my flesh;” for, “There is no rest in my bones,” is equivalent to this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He does not however repeat “from the face of Thine anger;” but states the cause of the anger of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is no rest in my bones from the face of my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For mine iniquities have lifted up my head; and are like a heavy burden too heavy for me to bear” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here too he has placed the cause first, and the effect afterwards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What consequence followed, and from what cause, he has told us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine iniquities have lift up mine head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no one is proud but the unrighteous man, whose head is lifted up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is “lifted up,” whose “head is lifted up on high” against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You heard when the lesson of the Book of Ecclesiasticus was read: “The beginning of pride is when a man departeth from God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who was the first to refuse to listen to the Commandment, “his head iniquity lifted up” against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because his iniquities have lifted up his head, what hath God done unto him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are “like a heavy burden, too heavy for me to bear”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the part of levity to lift up the head, just as if he who lifts up his head had nothing to carry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since therefore that which admits of being lifted up is light, it receives a weight by which it may be weighed down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “his mischief returns upon his own head, and his violent dealing comes down upon his own pate.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They are like a heavy burden, too heavy for me to bear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My wounds stink and are corrupt” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now he who has wounds is not perfectly sound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Add to this, that the wounds “stink and are corrupt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore do they “stink”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they are “corrupt:” now in what way this is explained in reference to human life, who doth not understand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let a man but have his soul’s sense of smelling sound, he perceives how foully sins stink.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The contrary to which stink of sin, is that savour of which the Apostle says, “We are the sweet savour of Christ unto God, in every place, unto them which be saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence is this, except from hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence is this, but from our “calling the Sabbath to remembrance”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is a different thing that we mourn over in this life, from that which we anticipate in the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which we mourn over is stench, that which we reckon upon is fragrance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were there not therefore such a perfume as that to invite us, we should never call the Sabbath to remembrance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since, by the Spirit, we have such a perfume, as to say to our Betrothed, “Because of the savour of Thy good ointments we will run after Thee;” we turn our senses away from our own unsavourinesses, and turning ourselves to Him, we gain some little breathing-time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But indeed, unless our evil deeds also did smell rank in our nostrils, we should never confess with those groans, “My wounds stink and are corrupt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wherefore? “from the face of my foolishness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the same cause that he said before, “from the face of my sins;” from that same cause he now says, “from the face of my foolishness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am troubled, I am bowed down even unto the end” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore was he “bowed down”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he had been “lifted up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou art “humble, thou shalt be exalted;” if thou exaltest thyself, thou shalt be “bowed down;” for God will be at no loss to find a weight wherewith to bow thee down.…Let him groan on these things; that he may receive the other; let him “call the Sabbath to remembrance,” that he may deserve to arrive at it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which the Jews used to celebrate was but a sign.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what thing was it the sign?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of that which he calls to remembrance, who saith, “I am troubled, and am bowed down even unto the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by even “unto the end”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even to death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I go mourning all the day long.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All day long,” that is, “without intermission.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By “all the day long,” he means, “all my life long.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But from what time hath he known it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the time that he began to “call the Sabbath to remembrance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so long as he “calls to remembrance” what he no longer possesses, wouldest thou not have him “go mourning”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the day long have I gone mourning.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my soul is filled with illusions, and there is no soundness in my flesh” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where there is the whole man, there there is soul and flesh both.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “soul is filled with illusions;” the flesh hath “no soundness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does there remain that can give joy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it not meet that one should “go mourning”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the day long have I gone mourning.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let mourning be our portion, until our soul be divested of its illusions; and our body be clothed with soundness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For true soundness is no other than immortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great however are the soul’s illusions, were I even to attempt to express, when would the time suffice me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whose soul is not subject to them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a brief particular that I will remind you of, to show how our soul is filled with illusions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The presence of those illusions sometimes scarcely permits us to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know not how to think of material objects without images, and such as we do not wish, rush in upon the mind; and we wish to go from this one to that, and to quit that for another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And sometimes you wish to return to that which you were thinking of before, and to quit that which you are now thinking of; and a fresh one presents itself to you; you wish to call up again what you had forgotten; and it does not occur to you; and another comes instead which you would not have wished for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where meanwhile was the one that you had forgotten?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For why did it afterwards occur to you, when it had ceased to be sought after; whereas, while it was being sought for, innumerable others, which were not desired, presented themselves instead of it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have stated a fact briefly; I have thrown out a kind of hint or suggestion to you, brethren, taking up which, you may yourselves suggest the rest to yourselves, and discover what it is to mourn over the “illusions” of our “soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath received therefore the punishment of illusion; he hath forfeited Truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For just as illusion is the soul’s punishment, so is Truth its reward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when we were set in the midst of these illusions, the Truth Itself came to us, and found us overwhelmed by illusions, took upon Itself our flesh, or rather took flesh from us; that is, from the human race.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He manifested himself to the eyes of the Flesh, that He might “by faith” heal those to whom He was going to reveal the Truth hereafter, that Truth might be manifested to the now healed eye.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is Himself “the Truth,” which He promised unto us at that time, when His Flesh was to be seen by the eye, that the foundation might be laid of that Faith, of which the Truth was to be the reward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was not Himself that Christ showed forth on earth; but it was His Flesh that He showed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had He showed Himself, the Jews would have seen and known Him; but had they “known Him, they would never have crucified the Lord of Glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps His disciples saw Him, when they said unto Him, “Show us the Father, and it sufficeth us;” and He, to show that it was not Himself that had been seen by them, added: “Have I been so long with you, and have ye not known Me, Philip?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that seeth Me, seeth the Father also.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then they saw Christ, wherefore did they yet seek for the Father?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if it were Christ whom they saw, they would have seen the Father also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They did not therefore yet see Christ, who desired that the Father should be shown unto them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To prove that they did not yet see Him, hear that, in another place, He promised it by way of reward, saying, “He who loveth Me, keepeth My commandments; and whoso loveth Me, shall be loved of My Father; and I will love Him and” (as if it were said to Him, “what wilt Thou give unto him, as Thou lovest him?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith), “I will manifest Myself unto him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then He promises this by way of a reward unto them that love Him, it is manifest that the vision of the Truth, promised to us, is of such a nature, that, when we have seen it, we shall no longer say, “My soul is filled with illusions.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am become feeble, and am bowed down greatly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who calls to mind the transcendent height of the Sabbath, sees how “greatly” he is himself “bowed down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he who cannot conceive what is that height of rest, sees not where he is at present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore another Psalm hath said, “I said in my trance, I am cast out of the sight of Thine eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For his mind being taken up thither, he beheld something sublime; and was not yet entirely there, where what he beheld was; and a kind of flash, as it were, if one may so speak, of the Eternal Light having glanced upon him, when he perceived that he was not yet arrived at this, which he was able after a sort to understand, he saw where he himself was, and how he was cramped and “bowed down” by human infirmities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he says, “I said in my trance, I am cast out of the sight of Thine eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such is that certain something which I saw in my trance, that thence I perceive how far off I am, who am not already there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was already there who said that he was “caught up into the third Heaven, and there heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he was recalled to us, in order that, as requiring to be made perfect, he might first mourn his infirmity, and afterwards be clothed with might.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet encouraged for the ministration of his office by having seen somewhat of those things, he goes on saying, “I heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then what use is it for you to ask, either of me or of any one, the “things which it is not lawful for man to utter.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If it was not lawful for him to utter them, to whom is it lawful to hear them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us however lament and groan in Confession; let us own where we are; let us “call the Sabbath to remembrance,” and wait with patience for what He has promised, who hath, in His own Person also, showed forth an example of patience to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am become feeble, and bowed down greatly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have roared with the groaning of my heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You observe the servants of God generally interceding with groaning; and the reason of it is asked, and there is nothing apparent, but the groaning of some servant of God, if indeed it does find its way at all to the ears of a person placed near him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is a secret groaning, which is not heard by man: yet if the thought of some strong desire has taken so strong hold of the heart, that the wound of the inner man finds expression in some uttered exclamation, the reason of it is asked; and a man says to himself, “Perhaps this is the cause of his groaning;” and, “Perhaps this or that hath befallen him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can determine, but He in whose Eyes and Ears he groaned?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he says, “I roared with the groaning of mine heart;” because if men ever hear a man’s groanings, they for the most part hear but the groaning of the flesh; they do not hear him who groans “with the groaning of his heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some one hath carried off his goods; he “roareth,” but not “with the groaning of his heart:” another because he has buried his son, another his wife; another because his vineyard has been injured by a hailstorm; another because his cask has turned sour; another because some one hath stolen his beast; another because he has suffered some loss; another because he fears some man who is his enemy: all these “roar” with the “groaning of the flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The servant of God, however, because he “roareth” from the recollection of the Sabbath, where the Kingdom of God is, which flesh and blood shall not possess, says, “I have roared with the groaning of my heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who observed and noticed the cause of his groaning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All my desire is before Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not before men who cannot see the heart, but it is before Thee that all my desire is open!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let your desire be before Him; and “the Father, who seeth in secret, shall reward thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is thy heart’s desire that is thy prayer; and if thy desire continues uninterrupted, thy prayer continueth also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not without a meaning did the Apostle say, “Pray without ceasing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are we to be “without ceasing” bending the knee, prostrating the body, or lifting up our hands, that he says, “Pray without ceasing”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or if it is in this sense that we say that we “pray,” this, I believe, we cannot do “without ceasing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is another inward kind of prayer without ceasing, which is the desire of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever else you are doing, if you do but long for that Sabbath, you do not cease to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If you would never cease to pray, never cease to long after it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The continuance of thy longing is the continuance of thy prayer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You will be ceasing to speak, if you cease to long for it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are those who have ceased to speak?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They of whom it is said, “Because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The freezing of charity is the silence of the heart; the burning of charity is the cry of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If love continues still you are still lifting up your voice; if you are always lifting up your voice, you are always longing after something; if always longing for something absent, you are calling “the Sabbath rest to remembrance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is important you should understand too before whom the “roaring of thine heart” is open.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then consider what sort of desires those should be, that are before the eyes of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should it be the desire for the death of our enemy? a thing which men flatter themselves they lawfully wish for?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sometimes we pray for what we ought not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us consider what they flatter themselves they pray for lawfully!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they pray that some person may die, and his inheritance come to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let those too, who pray for the death of their enemies, hear the Lord saying, “Pray for your enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them not pray for this, that their enemies may die; but rather pray for this, that they may be reclaimed; then will their enemies be dead; for from the time that they are reclaimed, henceforth they will be enemies no longer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all my desire is before Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if we suppose that our desire is before Him, and that yet that very “groaning” is not before Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How can that be, since our desire itself finds its expression in “groaning”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore follows, “And my groaning is not hid from Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From Thee indeed it is not hid; but from many men it is hid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The servant of God sometimes seems to be saying in humility, “And my groaning is not hid from Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes also he seems to smile.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is then that longing dead in his heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If however there is the desire within, there is the “groaning” also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It does not always find its way to the ears of man; but it never ceases to sound in the ears of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart is troubled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore is it troubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my courage hath failed me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Generally something comes upon us on a sudden; the “heart is troubled;” the earth quakes; thunder is sent from Heaven; a formidable attack is made upon us, or a horrible sound heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps a lion is seen on the road; the “heart is troubled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps robbers lie in wait for us; the “heart is troubled:” we are filled with a panic fear; from every quarter something excites anxiety.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “my courage hath failed me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what would be feared, did that courage still remain unmoved?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever bad tidings were brought, whatever threatened us, whatever sound was heard, whatever were to fall, whatever appeared horrible, would inspire no terror.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whence that trouble?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My courage faileth me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore hath my courage failed me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The light of mine eyes also is gone from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus Adam also could not see “the light of his eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the “light of his eyes” was God Himself, whom when he had offended, he fled to the shade, and hid himself among the trees of Paradise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shrunk in alarm from the face of God: and sought the shelter of the trees; thenceforth among the trees he had no more “the light of his eyes,” at which he had been wont to rejoice.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My lovers;” why should I henceforth speak of my enemies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My lovers and my neighbours drew nigh, and stood over against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand this that he saith, “Stood over against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they stood over against me, they fell against themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My lovers and my neighbours drew nigh and stood over against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us now recognise the words of the Head speaking; now let our Head in His Passion begin to dawn upon us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet again when the Head begins to speak, do not sever the Body from it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the Head would not separate itself from the words of the Body, should the Body dare to separate itself from the sufferings of the Head?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou suffer in Christ’s suffering: for Christ, as it were, sinned in thy infirmity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For just now He spoke of thy sins, as if speaking in His own Person, and called them His own.…To those who wished to be near His exaltation, yet thought not of His humility, He answered and said to them, “Can ye drink of the cup that I shall drink of?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those sufferings of the Lord then are our sufferings also: and were each individual to serve God well, to keep faith truly, to render to each their dues, and to conduct himself honestly among men, I should like to see if he does not suffer even that which Christ here details in the account of His Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My lovers and my neighbours drew nigh, and stood over against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my neighbours stood afar off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who were the “neighbours” that drew nigh, and who were those who stood afar off?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews were “neighbours” because “near kinsmen,” they drew near even when they crucified Him: the Apostles also were His “neighbours;” and they also “stood afar off,” that they might not have to suffer with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This may also be understood thus: “My friends,” that is, those who feigned themselves “My friends:” for they feigned themselves His friends, when they said, “We know that Thou teachest the way of God in truth;” when they wished to try Him, whether tribute ought to be paid to Cæsar; when He convinced them out of their own mouth, they wished to seem to be His friends.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But He needed not that any should testify of man, for He Himself knew what was in man;” so that when they spoke unto Him words of friendship, He answered them, “Why tempt ye Me, ye hypocrites?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My friends and my neighbours” then “drew near and stood over against me, and my neighbours stood afar off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You understand what I said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I called those neighbours who “drew nigh,” and at the same time “stood afar off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they “drew nigh” in the body, but “stood afar off” in their heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who were in the body so near to Him as those who lifted Him on the Cross?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who in heart so as those who blasphemed Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear this sort of distance described by the Prophet Isaiah; observe this nearness and distance at one and the same time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This people honours Me with their lips:” behold, with their body they draw near; “but their heart is far from Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same persons are at the same time “near” and “afar off” also: with their lips they are near, in heart afar off.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: However, because the Apostles also stood afar off, through fear, we understand it more simply and properly of them; so that we mean by it, that some drew near, and others stood afar off; since even Peter, who had followed more boldly than the rest, was still so far off, that being questioned and alarmed, he thrice denied the Lord, with whom he had promised to “be ready to die.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who afterwards that, from being afar off, he might be made to draw nigh, heard after the resurrection the question, “Lovest thou Me?” and said, “I love Thee;” and by so saying was brought “nigh,” even as by denying Him, he had become “far off;” till with the threefold confession of love, he had put away from him his threefold denial.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my neighbours stood afar off.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They also that sought after my soul were preparing violence against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is now plain who “sought after His soul;” viz. those who had not His soul, in that they were not in His Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who were “seeking after His soul,” were far removed from His soul; but they were “seeking it” to destroy it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For His soul may be “sought after” in a right way also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in another passage He finds fault with some persons, saying, “There is no man to care for My soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He finds fault with some for not seeking after His soul; and again, with others for seeking after it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is he that seeketh after His soul in the right way?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who imitates His sufferings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that sought after His soul in the wrong way?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even those who “prepared violence against Him,” and crucified Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goes on: “Those who sought after My faults had spoken vanity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “sought after My faults”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They sought after many things, and found them not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps He may have meant this: “They sought for criminal charges against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they sought for somewhat to say against Him, and “they found not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were seeking to find evil things to say of “the Good;” crimes of the Innocent; When would they find such things in Him, who had no sin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because they had to seek for sins in Him who had no sin, it remained for them to invent that which they could not find.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “those who sought after My faults have spoken vanity,” i.e., untruth, “and imagined deceit all the day long;” that is, they meditated treachery without intermission.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You know how atrocious false-witness was borne against the Lord, before He suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You know how atrocious false-witness was borne against Him, even after His resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those soldiers who watched His sepulchre of whom Isaiah spake, “I will appoint the wicked for His burial” (for they were wicked men, and would not speak the truth, and being bribed they disseminated a lie), consider what “vanity” they spake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They also were examined, and they said, “While we slept, His disciples came and stole Him away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This it is, “to speak vanity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they were sleeping, how could they know what had been done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith then, “But I as a deaf man heard not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who replied not to what He heard, did, as it were, not hear them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I as a deaf man heard not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he repeats the same things again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I became as a man that heareth not, and in whose mouth are no reproofs” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if He had nothing to say unto them, as if He had nothing wherewith to reproach them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had He not already reproached them for many things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had He not said many things, and also said, “Woe unto you, Scribes and Pharisees,” and many things besides?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet when He suffered, He said none of these things; not that He had not what to say, but He waited for them to fulfil all things, and that all the prophecies might be fulfilled of Him, of whom it had been said, “And as a sheep before her shearer is dumb, so openeth He not His mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It behoved Him to be silent in His Passion, though not hereafter to be silent in Judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He had come to be judged, then, who was hereafter coming to judge; and who was for this reason to come with great power to judge, that He had been judged in great humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For in Thee, O Lord, do I hope; Thou wilt hear, O Lord, my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if it were said to Him, “Wherefore openedst thou not thy mouth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore didst Thou not say, ‘Refrain’?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore didst Thou not rebuke the unrighteous, while hanging on the Cross?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goes on and says, “For in Thee, O Lord, do I hope; Thou, O Lord my God, wilt hear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He warns you what to do, should tribulation haply befall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For you seek to defend yourself, and perhaps your defence is not listened to by any one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then are you confounded, as if you had lost your cause; because you have none to defend or to bear testimony in your favour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Keep” but your “innocence” within, where no one can pervert thy cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: False-witness has prevailed against you before men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will it then prevail before God, where your cause has to be pleaded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When God shall be Judge, there shall be no other witness than your own conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the presence of a just judge, and of your own conscience, fear nothing but your own cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If you have not a bad cause, you will have no accuser to dread; no false-witness to confute, nor witness to the truth to look for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do but bring into court a good conscience, that you may say, “For in Thee, O Lord, do I hope; Thou, O Lord my God, wilt hear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I said, Let not mine enemies ever rejoice over me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when my feet slip, they magnify themselves against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again He returns to the infirmity of His Body: and again the Head takes heed of Its “feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Head is not in such a manner in Heaven, as to forsake what It has on earth; He evidently sees and observes us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sometimes, as is the way of this life, our feet are “turned aside,” and they slip by falling into some sin; there the tongues of the enemy rise up with the bitterest malignity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From this then we discern what they really had in view, even while they kept silence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then they speak with an unsparing harshness; rejoicing to have discovered what they ought to have grieved for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I said, Lest at any time my adversaries should rejoice over me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I said this indeed; and yet it was perhaps for my correction that Thou hast caused them to “magnify themselves against me, when my feet slipped;” that is to say, when I stumbled, they were elated, and said many things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For pity, not insult, was due from them to the weak; even as the Apostle speaks: “Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual restore such an one in the spirit of meekness;” and he combines the reason why: “considering thyself also, lest thou also be tempted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not such as these were the persons of whom He speaks: “And when my feet slipped, they rejoiced greatly against me;” but they were such as those of whom He says elsewhere: “They that hate me will rejoice if I fall.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I am prepared for the scourges” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Quite a magnificent expression; as if He were saying, “It was even for this that I was born; that I might suffer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He was not to be born, but from Adam, to whom the scourge is due.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But sinners are in this life sometimes not scourged at all, or are scourged less than their deserts: because the wickedness of their heart is given over as already desperate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those, however, for whom eternal life is prepared, must needs be scourged in this life: for that sentence is true: “My son, faint not under the chastening of the Lord, neither be weary when thou art rebuked of Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For whom the Lord loveth He chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not mine enemies therefore insult over me; let “them not magnify themselves;” and if my Father scourgeth me, “I am prepared for the scourge;” because there is an inheritance in store for me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not submit to the scourge: the inheritance is not bestowed upon thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “every son” must needs be scourged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So true it is that “every son” is scourged, that He spared not even Him who had no sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “I am prepared for the scourges.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my sorrow is continually before me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “sorrow” is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps, a sorrow for my scourge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, in good truth, my brethren, in good truth, let me say unto you, men do mourn for their scourges, not for the causes on account of which they are scourged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not such was the person here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, my brethren: If any person suffers any loss, he is more ready to say, “I did not deserve to suffer it,” than to consider why he suffered it, mourning the loss of money, not mourning over that of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hast sinned, mourn for the loss of thy inward treasure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast nothing in thy house, but perhaps thou art still more empty in heart; but if thine heart is full of its Good, even thy God, why dost thou not say, “The Lord gave, the Lord hath taken away; as it pleased the Lord was it done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessed be the Name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then was it that He was grieving?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it for the “scourging” wherewith He was scourged?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my sorrow” (says He) “is continually before me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if we were to say, “What sorrow? whence comes that sorrow?” he says: “For I declare mine iniquity; and I will have a care for my sin” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See here the reason for the sorrow!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not a sorrow occasioned by the scourge; not one for the remedy, not for the wound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the scourge is a remedy against sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, brethren; We are Christians, and yet if any one’s son dies, he mourns for him but does not mourn for him if he sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is then, when he sees him sinning, that he ought to make mourning for him, to lament over him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is then he should restrain him, and give him a rule to live by; should impose a discipline upon him: or if he has done so, and the other has not taken heed, then was the time when he ought to have been mourned over; then he was more fatally dead whilst living in luxury, than when, by death, he brought his luxury to its close: at that time, when he was doing such things in thine house, he was not only “dead, but he stank also.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things were worthy to be lamented, the others were such as might well be endured; those, I say, were tolerable, these worthy to be mourned over.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were to be mourned over in the same way that you have heard this person mourn over them: “For I declare mine iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will have a care for my sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not free from anxiety when you have confessed your sin, as if always able to confess thy sin, and to commit it again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou “declare thine iniquity in such a manner, as to have a care for thy sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “having a care of thy sin”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To have a care of thy wound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If you were to say, “I will have a care of my wound,” what would be meant by it, but I will do my endeavour to have it healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is “to have a care for one’s sin,” to be ever struggling, ever endeavouring, ever exerting one’s self, earnestly and zealously, to heal one’s wound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold! thou art from day to day mourning over thy sins; but perhaps thy tears indeed flow, but thy hands are unemployed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do alms, redeem thy sins, let the poor rejoice of thy bounty, that thou also mayest rejoice of the Grace of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is in want; so art thou in want also: he is in want at thy hands; so art thou also in want at God’s hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou despise one who needs thy aid; and shall God not despise thee when thou needest His?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou therefore supply the needs of him who is in want of thine aid; that God may supply thy needs within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the meaning of, “I will have a care for my sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will do all that ought to be done, to blot out and to heal my sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will have a care for my sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But mine enemies live” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are well off: they rejoice in worldly prosperity, while I am suffering, and “roaring with the groaning of my heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what way do His enemies “live,” in that He hath said of them already, that they have “spoken vanity”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear in another Psalm also: “Whose sons are as young plants; firmly rooted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But above He had said, “Whose mouth speaketh vanity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their daughters polished after the similitude of a temple: their garners full bursting forth more and more; their cattle fat, their sheep fruitful, multiplying in their streets; no hedge falling into ruin; no cry in their streets.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine enemies” then “live.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is their life; this life they praise; this they set their hearts upon: this they hold fast to their own ruin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They pronounce “the people that is in such a case” blessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what sayest thou, who “hast a care for thy sin”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sayest thou, who “confessest thine iniquity”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He says, “Blessed is the people whose God is the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But mine enemies live, and are strengthened against me, and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “hate me wrongfully”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They hate me, who wish their good, whereas were they simply requiting evil for evil, they would not be righteous; were they not to requite with good the good done to them, they would be ungrateful: they, however, who “hate wrongfully,” actually return evil for good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such were the Jews; Christ came unto them with good things; they requited Him evil for good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beware, brethren, of this evil; it soon steals upon us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one of you think himself to be far removed from the danger, because we said, “Such were the Jews.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should a brother, wishing your good, rebuke you, and you hate him, you are like them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And observe, how easily, how soon it is produced; and avoid an evil so great, a sin so easily committed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They also that render evil for good, were speaking evil of me, because I have pursued the thing that is just” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore was it that I was requited evil for good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “pursued after the thing that is just”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not forsaken it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That you might not always understand persecutio in a bad sense, He means by persecutus pursued after, thoroughly followed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because I have followed the thing that is just.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear also our Head crying with a lamentable voice in His Passion: “And they cast Me forth, Thy Darling, even as a dead man in abomination.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it not enough that He was “dead”? wherefore “in abomination” also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He was crucified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this death of the Cross was a great abomination in their eyes, as they did not perceive that it was spoken in prophecy, “Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He did not Himself bring death; but He found it here, propagated from the curse of the first man; and this same death of ours, which had originated in sin, He had taken upon Himself, and hung on the Tree.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest therefore some persons should think (as some of the Heretics think), that our Lord Jesus Christ had only a false body of flesh; and that the death by which He made satisfaction on the Cross was not a real death, the Prophet notices this, and says, “Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shows then that the Son of God died a true death, the death which was due to mortal flesh: lest if He were not “accursed,” you should think that He had not truly died.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since that death was not an illusion, but had descended from that original stock, which had been derived from the curse, when He said, “Ye shall surely die:” and since a true death assuredly extended even to Him, that a true life might extend itself to us, the curse of death also did extend to Him, that the blessing of life might extend even unto us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they cast Me forth, Thy Darling, even as a dead man in abomination.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Forsake me not, O Lord; O my God, depart not from me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us speak in Him, let us speak through Him (for He Himself intercedeth for us), and let us say, “Forsake me not, O Lord my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet He had said, “My God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My God! why hast Thou forsaken Me?” and He now says, “O My God, depart not from Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He does not forsake the body, did He forsake the Head?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whose words then are these but the First Man’s?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To show then that He carried about Him a true body of flesh derived from him, He says, “My God, My God why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God had not forsaken Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He does not forsake Thee, who believest in Him, could the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost, One God, forsake Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He had transferred to Himself the person of the First Man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know by the words of an Apostle, that “our old man is crucified with Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We should not, however, be divested of our old nature, had He not been crucified “in weakness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was to this end that He came that we may be renewed in Him, because it is by aspiration after Him, and by following the example of His suffering, that we are renewed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore that was the cry of infirmity; that cry, I mean, in which it was said, “Why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence was it said in that passage above, “the words of mine offences.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if He were saying, These words are transferred to My Person from that of the sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Depart not from me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Make haste to help me, Lord of my salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is that very “salvation,” Brethren, concerning which, as the Apostle Peter saith, “Prophets have enquired diligently,” and though they have enquired diligently, yet have not found it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they searched into it, and foretold of it; while we have come and have found what they sought for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And see, we ourselves too have not as yet received it; and after us shall others also be born, and shall find, what they also shall not receive, and shall pass away, that we may, all of us together, receive the “penny of salvation in the end of the day,” with the Prophets, the Patriarchs, and the Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For you know that the hired servants, or labourers, were taken into the vineyard at different times; yet did they all receive their wages on an equal footing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Apostles, then, and Prophets, and Martyrs, and ourselves also, and those who will follow us to the end of the world, it is in the End itself that we are to receive everlasting salvation; that beholding the face of God, and contemplating His Glory, we may praise Him for ever, free from imperfection, free from any punishment of iniquity, free from every perversion of sin: praising Him; and no longer longing after Him, but now clinging to Him for whom we used to long to the very end, and in whom we did rejoice, in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we shall be in that City, where God is our Bliss, God is our Light, God is our Bread, God is our Life; whatever good thing of ours there is, at being absent from which we now grieve, we shall find in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Him will be that “rest,” which when we “call to remembrance” now, we cannot choose but grieve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that is the “Sabbath” which we “call to remembrance;” in the recollection of which, so great things have been said already; and so great things ought to be said by us also, and ought never to cease being said by us, not with our lips indeed, but in our heart: for therefore do our lips cease to speak, that we may cry out with our hearts.
